topNews.title: Claude Code Opus 4.7 keeps checking on malware
topNews.rawTextSnippet: So during development, at every task I start, I see a line like this: `Own bug file — not malware.` It seems that it's obsessively checking if it's working on malware production. In another situation where I was working on a parser of a HTML document with JS, it refused because it believed…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Claude Code Opus 4.7 is consistently verifying its tasks to ensure they are not related to malware.  
• Why it matters: This vigilance enhances security and trust in the software's development process.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this feature to promote safer coding practices and attract security-conscious clients.

topNews.title: Show HN: Sfsym – Export Apple SF Symbols as Vector SVG/PDF/PNG
topNews.rawTextSnippet: I found myself reaching for SF Symbols' 'Copy Image As…' quite often during agentic design sessions, so I made a command-line tool that the agent can use by itself. It exports Apple SF Symbols as SVG, PDF, or PNG. The vector paths come directly from macOS's symbol renderer. Inter…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A developer created a command-line tool, Sfsym, to export Apple SF Symbols as SVG, PDF, or PNG.  
• Why it matters: This tool streamlines the design process by allowing easier access to vector graphics from macOS's symbol renderer.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for further development of design tools that enhance productivity for designers using Apple’s ecosystem.

dailyBrief: Today, Claude Design's latest updates, including the release of Claude Opus 4.7, emphasize enhanced intelligence and performance metrics, crucial for businesses relying on AI for operational efficiency. Notably, the new version continues to monitor for malware, addressing security concerns that are paramount for organizations integrating AI solutions. In a related development, researchers successfully replicated Anthropic's Mythos findings using public models, highlighting the growing accessibility of advanced AI capabilities. Meanwhile, Atlassian's new data collection policy appears to favor larger clients, raising concerns about equitable access to AI tools for smaller businesses. These developments underscore the need for companies to stay agile and informed as the AI landscape evolves rapidly, impacting competitive dynamics and operational strategies.
